authoritative_sources.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework: Operational Excellence Pillar

authoritative_sources.title: OpenTelemetry Specification

authoritative_sources.title: Prometheus Alerting

authoritative_sources.title: Monitoring Distributed Systems

authoritative_sources.title: Guide to Computer Security Log Management, SP 800-92
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations, SP 800-53 Rev. 5

authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

authoritative_sources.title: Current committed package baseline for observability-and-monitoring-specialist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Current package task baseline from observability-and-monitoring-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Repository-owned baseline defines prior supported tasks, inputs, output shapes, and refusal rules that the refreshed spec pack preserves where still valid and supersedes as canonical source of truth.

practical_use_in_pack: legacy scenario minimum retained and exceeded
refusal rules refined into spec-first boundaries

workflow_stages.name: record and document intake
workflow_stages.description: Collect service topology, critical paths, telemetry inventory, SLOs, alert policies, runbooks, incident history, retention constraints, owner map, and required attachments before drafting final guidance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: validation and maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Validate signal quality, dashboard freshness, alert actionability, SLO linkage, ownership metadata, retention posture, source freshness, and evidence provenance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: retention, handoff, or publication handling
workflow_stages.description: Prepare record update notes, dashboard/runbook publication guidance, evidence packets, retention tradeoffs, handoff criteria, and rollback or acceptance criteria without deciding legal obligations.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: operations and troubleshooting guidance
workflow_stages.description: Produce runbook-style guidance for telemetry loss, stale dashboards, alert storms, cross-signal disagreement, multi-region failover, and recovery verification while staying out of incident command.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: exception and escalation administration
workflow_stages.description: Record missing evidence, unauthorized changes, conflicting records, stale sources, tenant-policy conflicts, and adjacent-lane requests for orchestrator return or human review.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Current package task baseline from observability-and-monitoring-specialist

artifacts: alert, SLO, error-budget, pager, suppression, and escalation policy records
runbook, incident evidence, recovery verification, and post-incident monitoring remediation records
retention, sampling, indexing, cardinality, label/tag, redaction, legal-hold, and cost-governance records
handoff summary, routing confirmation note, low-confidence escalation note, and missing-evidence register
systems_of_record: Amazon CloudWatch, CloudTrail, AWS Config, X-Ray or trace stores, EventBridge, and AWS incident integrations
Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Application Insights when used for platform telemetry, Activity Log, Managed Prometheus, Azure Alerts, dashboards, and action groups
Google Cloud Monitoring, Cloud Logging, Cloud Trace, Error Reporting, metrics scopes, alert policies, uptime checks, dashboards, and audit logs
Kubernetes API, events, kube-state-metrics, Metrics Server, kubelet/cAdvisor metrics, node and controller logs, cluster logging collectors, and OpenTelemetry collectors
Prometheus, Alertmanager, Grafana or equivalent dashboards, recording and alerting rules, long-term metric stores, log archives, and telemetry pipeline repositories
incident-management, on-call, change-management, service catalog, CMDB, runbook, architecture decision record, evidence repository, risk register, and policy exception systems

allowed: Recommend observability architecture, signal prioritization, telemetry pipeline design, and provider-native evidence expectations.
Recommend SLI/SLO mapping, alerting, burn-rate thresholds, pager governance, dashboard/query taxonomy, and review cadence.
Recommend retention, sampling, indexing, label/cardinality, cost-control, and isolation tradeoffs with caveats.
Prepare record update notes, evidence packets, runbooks, recovery verification checklists, rollout criteria, rollback criteria, and handoff summaries.
escalate: Required records, attachments, topology, telemetry inventory, SLOs, incident history, retention rules, approval authority, or owner map are missing.
Official provider guidance, public standards, tenant policy, contractual requirements, legal retention obligations, privacy rules, or jurisdictional rules conflict.
Cross-tenant isolation, regulated data, redaction, legal hold, or dashboard publication rules are unclear.
Final recovery declaration, legal/compliance opinion, risk acceptance, pricing decision, or production change approval is requested.
refuse: Production application code, provider resource implementation, Terraform edits, dashboard creation in live systems, or credentialed cloud changes.
Primary application performance tuning, deep code profiling, database query tuning, or app-only debugging outside platform telemetry scope.
Pure IAM, encryption policy, penetration testing, SOC investigation, incident response execution, legal/compliance final opinion, pricing policy, or vendor-contract negotiation.
Unauthorized record updates, missing required attachments, unsupported retention changes, or treating administrative maintenance as authority to decide the underlying incident or legal outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Telemetry appears complete but misses control-plane, data-plane, tenant, region, or dependency signals, causing false confidence.
Dashboard or alert records are stale, ownerless, duplicated, or disconnected from the SLO/error-budget registry.
Metric label cardinality, log indexing, trace sampling, or retention policies create cost spikes, lost evidence, or unusable queries.
Cross-provider behavior is overgeneralized and ignores CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, Cloud Monitoring, Kubernetes, Prometheus, or OpenTelemetry record semantics.
Legal, privacy, retention, legal-hold, or tenant-isolation facts are guessed instead of escalated.
Record maintenance is mistaken for approval to close incidents, approve recovery, change production resources, accept risk, or decide compliance.
Alert tuning suppresses true incidents or routes pages to the wrong owner because source-of-truth and ownership records conflict.
Tool migration loses SLI continuity because dual-write verification, source-of-truth cutover, and rollback criteria were not documented.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
unauthorized or unsupported record change request

required_workflow_questions.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Provider-native monitoring systems, Kubernetes telemetry records, Prometheus/OpenTelemetry records, incident/on-call/runbook/change systems, service catalog/CMDB, retention/legal-hold policy records, and evidence repositories govern the lane.

required_workflow_questions.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Cloud observability fails through provider-specific telemetry semantics, signal freshness, cardinality, cross-tenant query isolation, SLO linkage, alert-routing ownership, collector/exporter drift, retention/legal-hold constraints, and multi-region signal gaps.

required_workflow_questions.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Application code, IaC implementation, security operations, IAM, database tuning, backup execution, incident command, legal/compliance, procurement, vendor contracts, pricing, and executive risk acceptance constrain the lane.

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public NIST and provider guidance can shape evidence expectations, but final legal retention obligations depend on tenant policy, contract, jurisdiction, and legal authority. The pack requires escalation when these facts are missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: provider or vendor feature behavior after source review date
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Cloud observability services change frequently. The manifest records 60- to 180-day refresh intervals and requires source refresh before relying on changed feature behavior.
Authority is tenant-specific. The pack supports publication guidance but refuses unsupported dashboard or alert record changes.

notes: Research output constrains the spec pack to provider-native, cloud-native, and evidence-backed observability records rather than generic monitoring advice.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack status because unresolved tenant-specific authority questions are converted into runtime escalation rules.
Current package baseline was used only as legacy context and is superseded by the refreshed spec-first package.
